registry.title: Information elements used in NetScaler
registry.note: For the IPFIX schema, please see .

registry.title: NetScaler Information Elements
registry.note: Below are some of the abbreviations used in the description of some of the information elements. * NS: Netscaler * MSI: Multi-Stream ICA. In this mode each ICA session uses 4 different connections and traffic is prioritized differently across each connection * WanOpt: WAN Optimizer appliance, like Citrix Cloudbridge * XA: XenApp - A Citrix product to publish and remotely access applications from a portal * XD: XenDesktop - A Citrix product to publish and remotely access desktops from a portal * EUEM: End User Experience Monitoring - A capability which can be enabled on an ICA session, and enables metrics that reflect the end user experience to be captured * RTT: Round Trip Time. * CB: Citrix CloudBridge

description.paragraph: The IPv6 source address in the IP packet header
The IPv6 destination address in the IP packet header

Unique session GUID associated with the ICA Session. If XA 6.5 or older is deployed, NetScaler will generate the GUID currently. Therefore every time a session reconnects, there is a new GUID. If XD 5.6, XD 7.0 and above are deployed, the GUID is generated by ICA Broker/host. The same GUID is used across reconnects.
MSI Connection GUID. It is unique for a MSI Connection session. All Connections in this MSI Session share the same client cookie.

This ID is used to collate connections across multiple devices over which an ICA session can be monitored. The Citrix device that terminates this connection sets this value and the other devices in the chain forward it. It is a combination of NetScaler MAC address, NetScaler process start timestamp and connection setup timestamp.
The number of octets since the previous report (if any) in incoming packets for this flow at the Observation Point. The number of octets includes IP header(s) and IP payload.
The number of incoming packets since the previous report (if any) for this Flow at the Observation Point.
Information Element can be used to distinguish between different Flows if Flow Keys such as IP addresses and port numbers are not reported or are reported in separate records.
The absolute timestamp of the first packet of this Flow
The absolute timestamp of the last packet of this Flow

application layer flags, for use between the exporter and collector to indicate various Layer-7 events and types like the direction of the flow (client-in, svc-out, etc.), http version, NetScaler cache served responses, SSL, compression, TCP buffering, and many more.
The total size of HTTP response
Time elapsed in microseconds between receiving of request from the client and receiving the first byte of response from server
Time elapsed in microseconds between receiving of request from the client and receiving the last byte of response from server
Timestamp when the first byte of request was received from client at the NetScaler
Timestamp when the first byte of request was forwarded to server from the NetScaler
Timestamp when the first byte of response was received from server at the NetScaler
Timestamp when the first byte of response was forwarded to client from the NetScaler
Timestamp when the last byte of request was received from client at the NetScaler
Timestamp when the last byte of request was forwarded to server from the NetScaler
Timestamp when the last byte of response was received from server at the NetScaler
Timestamp when the last byte of response was forwarded to client from the NetScaler
SQL response status
SQL response length

These flags indicate properties of the ICA session. Values are indicated as follows:0x00000004 = MSI enabled. 0x00000008 = MSI Secondary. 0x00000010 = Seamless Session. 0x00000020 = ICA Compression disabled. 0x00000040 = Session GUID from XA/XD. 0x00000080 = EUEM Enabled on Session. 0x00000100 = Client supports EUEM. 0x00001000 = WanOpt on clientside of this NS. 0x00002000 = WanOpt on Serverside of this NS.
Not used currently
Not used currently.
It might be required for the Appflow collector to know if certain features are enabled on NetScaler. This flag indicates features enabled on NetScaler. 0x0000000000000001: Cache Redirection
The IPv4 source address in the IP packet header.

The index of the IP interface where packets of this Flow are being received. The value matches the value of managed object 'ifIndex' as defined in [RFC2863]. Note that ifIndex values are not assigned statically to an interface and that the interfaces may be renumbered every time the device's management system is re-initialized, as specified in [RFC2863]
The IPv4 destination address in the IP packet header.

The index of the IP interface where packets of this Flow are being sent. The value matches the value of managed object 'ifIndex' as defined in [RFC2863]. Note that ifIndex values are not assigned statically to an interface and that the interfaces may be renumbered every time the device's management system is re-initialized, as specified in [RFC2863]

An identifier of an Observation Point that is unique per Observation Domain. It is RECOMMENDED that this identifier is also unique per IPFIX Device. Typically, this Information Element is used for limiting the scope of other Information Elements.

An identifier of an Exporting Process that is unique per IPFIX Device. Typically, this Information Element is used for limiting the scope of other Information Elements. Note that process identifiers are typically assigned dynamically. The Exporting Process may be re-started with a different ID.

An identifier of an Observation Domain that is locally unique to an Exporting Process. The Exporting Process uses the Observation Domain ID to uniquely identify to the Collecting Process the Observation Domain where Flows were metered. It is RECOMMENDED that this identifier is also unique per IPFIX Device. A value of 0 indicates that no specific Observation Domain is identified by this Information Element. Typically, this Information Element is used for limiting the scope of other Information Elements.
The TCP RTT of the flow in milliseconds since the time last record was sent
At Layer-7, the four flows of a transaction between client and server (client-to-NS, NS-to-Server, Server-to-NS, NS-to-Client) are tied together using the transaction ID.
The two flows of a TCP connection are tied together with a connection ID
Timestamp when the syslog (contained in the syslog record) was generated

Each named entity in the NetScaler is associated with an id. The name to id mapping is sent in the appname mapping template to the collector. Other records only contain the id of the entity and the corresponding name is stored by the collector and used when required. If a new entity gets added or an entity gets removed or modified, the incarnation number changes which indicates the collector to use the appname mapping record to update its database
The id of a named entity
SQL login flags

In a html page, the main page transaction is associated with all its embedded object transactions. Each such embedded object transaction record contains the transaction Id of the main page so that a parent link to the main transaction can be created. This is used in generating a waterfall chart model depicting the various timing information of the entire page loading.
The above transaction ID is unique within the process. Hence the exporting process ID of the main page is also required.

Netscaler uses application templates that groups a set of entities which can be exported and imported when needed. This Information Element exportes the ID of the application template to which the entity belongs.
The RTT of the client, in milliseconds, is exported in the server side records
Client IP address received from client-side connection on NetScaler
Absolute timestamp of beginning of ICA session update
Absolute timestamp of end of ICA session update

ID of this ICA channel, as described below: 1-Client Audio Mapping. 2-Seamless Windows screen update data. 3-Citrix Control Virtual channel. 4-End User experience Montitoring. 5-Citrix Flash Redirection. 6-USB Redirection. 7-Citrix browser Acceleration. 8-Smartcard. 9-Clipboard. 10-License Management. 11-Program Neighbourhood. 12-Remote Windows screen update data. 13-Video server video(not Thinwire video. 14-Client COM port mapping. 15-Client drive mapping. 16-Citrix Windows Multimedia Redirection. 17-Client Management (Auto Client Update). 18-Printer Mapping for non-spooling client (Thin client devices). 19-Printer Mapping for non-spooling client (Thin client devices). 20-Printer mapping for spooling clients. 21-Printer Mapping for non-spooling client (Thin client devices). 22-Printer Mapping for non-spooling client (Thin client devices).
Number of bytes transferred on this channel
Refer to the description of netscalerIcaChannelId1
Number of bytes transferred on this channel
Refer to the description of netscalerIcaChannelId1
Number of bytes transferred on this channel
Refer to the description of netscalerIcaChannelId1
Number of bytes transferred on this channel
Refer to the description of netscalerIcaChannelId1
Number of bytes transferred on this channel
The time elapsed between the launch of an application and when it started running
The time when an application started on the server
The time when the ICA session ended

ICA based RTT estimate in ms. Calculated by ICA client by sending a simulated keystroke packet to server and calculating response time for that packet. This value depends on EUEM being enabled. Without EUEM it will be 0.
Number of bytes received on client ICA connection
Number of bytes received on server ICA connection
This is set to the averaged TCP RTT value over this appflow interval on the client ICA connection. It is reported in milliseconds.
This is set to the averaged TCP RTT value over this appflow interval on the server connection. It is reported in milliseconds.
The time when the application was terminated
This value indicates the variance or jitter in the TCP RTT for the client side connection, over an appflow interval. It is indicated in milliseconds.
This value indicates the variance or jitter in the TCP RTT for the server side connection, over an appflow interval. It is indicated in milliseconds.
The time when the ICA session TCP was setup at NetScaler
The process ID of the application launched on the server
A number used in conjunction with clientcookie to identify primary connection and tie up streams of a MSI connection.

A network update record that contains the ICA connection statistics for a defined appflow interval is sent once every appflow interval. This Information Element contains the timestamp when the collection stats in this record began.

A network update record that contains the ICA connection statistics for a defined appflow interval is sent once every appflow interval. This Information Element contains the timestamp when the collection stats in this record ended.

This value indicates the TCP RTT smoothed over the lifetime of the client side connection. It reflects a longer term view of the RTT compared to the Appflow interval based RTT. It is indicated in milliseconds.

This value indicates the TCP RTT smoothed over the lifetime of the server side connection. It reflects a longer term view of the RTT compared to the Appflow interval based RTT. It is indicated in milliseconds.
Indicates time taken by Netscaler to process this client side packet (NS introduced processing delay). It is indicated in milliseconds.
Indicates time taken by Netscaler to process this server side packet (NS introduced processing delay). It is indicated in milliseconds.
Indicates a portion of the ICA RTT measurement - time delay introduced at the Host while processing the packet. It is indicated in milliseconds.
Layer 7 latency measured using ICA probes and responses sent between Receiver and the Host, on client side connection.
Layer 7 latency measured using ICA probes and responses sent between Receiver and the Host, on server side connection.
The client connection id is unique within a process. Hence the process id of the client connection is also passed to make the complete set unique.

When a request hits CR vserver and is redirected to the cache server and a cache miss happens, the cache sends a the request to the origin server. This request mostly comes back to the NS. This ID is used to link the cache request with the actual client request on the collector.
Number of Gif images scanned in the main page as part of Front End Optimization feature.
Number of GIF images optimized in the main page as part of Front End Optimization feature
Decrement in size as a result of GIF image optimization in the main page as part of Front End Optimization feature
Increase in size as a result of GIF image optimization in the main page as part of Front End Optimization feature
Number of GIF images converted to PNG in the main page as part of Front End Optimization feature
Number of JPEG images scanned in the main page as part of Front End Optimization feature
Number of JPEG images optimized in the main page as part of Front End Optimization feature
Decrease in size as a result of JPEG optimization in the main page as part of Front End Optimization feature
Increase in size as a result of JPEG optimization in the main page as part of Front End Optimization feature
Number of JavaScripts scanned in the main page as part of Front End Optimization feature
Number of JavaScripts optimized in the main page as part of Front End Optimization feature
Decrease in size as a result of JavaScript optimization in the main page as part of Front End Optimization feature
Increase in size as a result of JavaScript optimization in the main page as part of Front End Optimization feature
Number of JavaScripts inlined in the main page as part of Front End Optimization feature
Number of JavaScripts moved in the main page as part of Front End Optimization feature
Number of JavaScripts minified in the main page as part of Front End Optimization feature
Number of CSS objects scanned in the main page as part of Front End Optimization feature
Number of CSS objects optimized in the main page as part of Front End Optimization feature
Decrease in size as a result of CSS optimization in the main page as part of Front End Optimization feature
Increase in size as a result of CSS optimization in the main page as part of Front End Optimization feature
Number of CSS objects inlined in the main page as part of Front End Optimization feature
Number of CSS objects moved in the main page as part of Front End Optimization feature
Number of CSS objects minified in the main page as part of Front End Optimization feature
Number of images inlined in CSS in the main page as part of Front End Optimization feature
Number of CSS objects combined in the main page as part of Front End Optimization feature
Number of CSS objects imported to link in the main page as part of Front End Optimization feature
Total number of objects scanned in the main page as part of Front End Optimization feature
Total number of objects optimized in the main page as part of Front End Optimization feature
Total decrease in the size as a result of all the optimizations in the main page as part of Front End Optimization feature
Total increase inthe size as a result of all the optimizations in the main page as part of Front End Optimization feature
Change in size as a result of HTML minification in the main page as part of Front End Optimization feature
As part of the lazy-load feature of Front End Optimization, a javascript is added to the page. This field indicates the size of the script.
Number of PNG images scanned in the main page as part of Front End Optimization feature
Number of PNG images optimized in the main page as part of Front End Optimization feature
Decrease in size as a result of PNG optimization in the main page as part of Front End Optimization feature
Increase in size as a result of PNG optimization in the main page as part of Front End Optimization feature
Total number of images scanned in the main page as part of Front End Optimization feature
Total number of images optimized in the main page as part of Front End Optimization feature
Total decrease in size as a result of image optimizations in the main page as part of Front End Optimization feature
Total increase in size as a result of imate optimizations in the main page as part of Front End Optimization feature
Total number of images inlined in the main page as part of Front End Optimization feature
Total number of images shrinked in the main page as part of Front End Optimization feature
Total number of images lazy loaded in the main page as part of Front End Optimization feature
The type of Front End Optimizations done on this embedded object. 0x01: Data Inlined. 0x02: Data moved. 0x04: Domain Shraded. 0x08: Cache Extended. 0x10: Import to Link
The original size of the embedded object.
The content type of this embedded object. 3: JavaScripts. 4: CSS. 7: Images

The source port identifier in the transport header. For the transport protocols UDP, TCP, and SCTP, this is the source port number given in the respective header. This field MAY also be used for future transport protocols that have 16-bit source port identifiers.

The destination port identifier in the transport header. For the transport protocols UDP, TCP, and SCTP, this is the destination port number given in the respective header. This field MAY also be used for future transport protocols that have 16-bit destination port identifiers.
The value of this Information Element is always a sequence of 0x00 values
Status of HTTP response

This indicates the type of client on which the ICA client/receiver is running. 0x0001 = Citrix DOS client. 0x0003 = Citrix console. 0x0004 = Citrix Text terminals. 0x0007 = Citrix MVGA terminals. 0x0008 = Citrix JAVA client. 0x000A = Citrix EPOC client. 0x000B = Citrix OS/2 client. 0x000E = Citrix DOS32 client. 0x1f09 = Citrix WinCE client. 0x0051 = Citrix Unix client. 0x0052 = Citrix Mac client. 0x0053 = Citrix iPhone client. 0x0054 = Citrix Android client. 0x0055 = Citrix Blackberry client. 0x0056 = Citrix Windows Metro client. 0x0057 = Citrix Windows Phone client. 0x0058 = Citrix Blackberry playbook client. 0x0101 = Citrix HTML5 client. 0x0105 = Citrix JAVA client. 0x4000 = Client requires license number. 0x8000 = Terminal Client. 0x80ac = WinCE WYSE client. 0x80e1 = ThisOS WYSE.
This value is 0 for non-MSI sessions and non-CB cases. This priority is assigned by CB for QoS. The range is [0-3]. 0 : Highest Priority Connection. 3: Least priority connection.
Indicates how the application termination happened, eg: User closed the app, session termination, abort etc. This field currently carries only value 0 and the support is yet to be implemented.
Number of packets retransmitted on clientside connection
Number of packets retransmitted on serverside connection
Indicates what type of launcher was used for the ICA client. PNA = 1 (Program Neighorhood Agent). WI = 2 (Web Interface). PNC = 3 (Old Program Neighborhood Classic). Others = 4.
Indicates the launch mechanism. PN = 1, PNA = 2, WI = 3, MSAM = 4, Others = 5
Number of times TCP window became 0 on client side connection, during this appflow interval.
Number of times TCP window became 0 on server side connection, during this appflow interval.
Number of times retransmit timer was hit on client side connection, during this appflow interval.
Number of times retransmit timer was hit on server side connection, during this appflow interval.

In Internet Protocol version 4 (IPv4), this is carried in the Protocol field. In Internet Protocol version 6 (IPv6), this is carried in the Next Header field in the last extension header of the packet
The IP version field in the IP packet header
The value of this Information Element is always a sequence of 0x00 values
Priority of the syslog message being logged

TCP control bits observed for packets of this Flow. The information is encoded in a set of bit fields. For each TCP control bit, there is a bit in this set. A bit is set to 1 if any observed packet of this Flow has the corresponding TCP control bit set to 1. A value of 0 for a bit indicates that the corresponding bit was not set in any of the observed packets of this Flow.
The direction of the Flow observed at the Observation Point. There are only two values defined. 0x00: ingress flow 0x01: egress flow
The type of database request
The database protocol being used
Number of times this session was restarted by ICA.
Not used currently
The hop count of the current device in the connection chain from client to server (see connection chain id for more details)
HTTP request URL
Value of Cookie header present in HTTP request
Value of Referer header present in HTTP request
The request method in HTTP request
Value of Host header in HTTP request
The User Agent string as seen in HTTP request header
The syslog message generated on Netscaler
Name of the entity configured on Netscaler for which the name-to-id mapping is being sent in the current record
Database username
DB database name
DB client host name
DB request string
Status of the response as indicated in the Database response
Name of the template to which the current entity belongs (see netscalerAppTemplateID)
The Content Type string as seen in the HTTP header
Value of the Authorization HTTP header
Value of the Via HTTP header
value of the X-Forwarded-For HTTP header
Value of the Location HTTP response header
value of the Set-Cookie HTTP response header
value of the Set-Cookie2 HTTP response header
If the connection is over VPN, the AAA username for the session
Client receiver software version number.
Hostname of ICA Client.
Username for the ICA session
Domain that ICA client is joined to.
Name of XA/XD server that client is accessing.
ICA application name
Full path of the ICA application executable
HTTP domain name
The URL of the embedded object that was moved, inlined or converted from import to link etc. as part of Front End Optimization.
The NTP timestamp when the HTML page becomes interactive to the user
The timestamp when the page starts loading
The timestamp when the page completely renders
The timestamp when page rendering begins
